perSource.reviewNotes: AFSA public register page remains live and lists Bybit Limited with active licence AFSA-A-LA-2024-0027 from 2024-09-25 plus six regulated activities.
AFSA regulated-activity page remains live and defines Operating a Digital Asset Trading Facility.
AFSA stablecoin-fees notice remains live and names Bybit Limited as inaugural signatory/provider in the pilot.
AIFC/AFSA forged-licence alert remains live and describes impersonation using a forged older licence, not disciplinary action against Bybit Limited.
Bybit Kazakhstan Privacy Policy remains live and states the platform is provided by Bybit Limited.
Bybit Kazakhstan Risk Disclosure remains live and states the platform is provided by Bybit Limited.
Local restricted-countries policy remains live; Kazakhstan is not listed. Local excluded-jurisdiction list differs materially from the global page, which is relevant to routing.
Global restricted-countries policy remains live; Kazakhstan is not listed. Global excluded-jurisdiction list differs materially from the local Kazakhstan page.
perSource.originalLimitations: Recovered as snippet rather than fully parsed article body.

perSource.reviewNotes: Getting-started page remains live and still describes registration, KYC, one-click buy using KZT fiat balance, spot, earn and launchpad on Bybit Kazakhstan.
Registration guide remains live and still says email/mobile registration is available and that only KZ residents can register; this now conflicts with the newer foreign-nationals announcement.
Account settings FAQ remains live and still confirms referral-code entry at sign-up, email registration fallback, app-store download links, and restricted-country login rules.

English parser was unstable, but the same official article in Russian and Kazakh locales remained live and confirmed mandatory individual KYC, accepted identity documents, proof-of-address rules, and citizenship-based territorial language.
Individual KYC guide remains live and confirms standard verification components, Kazakhstan proof-of-address requirement, and supported-products access after verification.
Business KYC FAQ remains live and confirms accepted identity documents, proof-of-address timing, compliance contact email, and business-KYC restrictions.
KYC-failure article remains live and confirms accepted/unaccepted identity and address documents and unsupported-country rejection reasons.
P2P FAQ remains live and confirms P2P availability on website/app and KYC requirement.

Local P2P USDT/KZT market page remains live and shows KZT offers and bank examples, but detailed payment-method scope should be read with care because other official help content narrows supported setup options.
Fiat-deposit guide remains live and still shows only KZT via bank transfer, with IIN/BIN and BCC-account requirements.
Fiat-deposit FAQ remains live and still confirms KZT-only, bank-transfer-only, Kazakhstan-verified-user eligibility, and strict KYC-matching/no-third-party payment rules.
Fiat-withdrawal guide remains live and still shows only KZT via bank transfer, with first-deposit requirement for withdrawal setup.
Fiat-withdrawal FAQ remains live and still confirms KZT-only, bank-transfer/IBAN-linked withdrawal accounts, Kazakhstan-verified-user eligibility, and strict beneficiary/IIN/BIN matching.
Local one-click-buy via fiat-balance article remains live and still documents KZT balance purchases for BTC, ETH, USDT and USDC.
Perpetual/expiry trading-page guide remains live and still documents derivatives page features on Bybit Kazakhstan.
Inverse perpetual/expiry FAQ remains live and still documents perpetual vs expiry contracts and derivatives access prerequisites.
Futures getting-started guide remains live and still documents isolated/cross/portfolio margin and default cross-mode 10× order entry.
Spot-margin FAQ remains live and still states cross and portfolio margin are supported while isolated margin is not.
Spot-margin getting-started guide remains live and still documents cross/portfolio setup and default enablement within supported margin modes.
Risk-limit article remains live and still states that maximum leverage, IMR, MMR and position limits change with risk-parameter adjustments.
Options introduction remains live and still states Bybit Kazakhstan offers USDT-margined and USDT-settled options.

Bybit Card application guide remains live and documents local card onboarding through bybit.kz, but availability is limited by supported countries, stronger KYC, address checks, and phased physical rollout.
Rewards-type article remains live and confirms reward structures across Bybit Kazakhstan products, including card-related rewards and trading/fiat fee savers.

Official announcement page remains live by title/date; parser exposed only the shell, but official search/index snippets still state that Bybit Kazakhstan accepts users with non-Kazakhstan citizenship for registration and KYC.

perSource.reviewNotes: Announcement index remains live and still lists KZT trading pairs launch and the foreign-nationals announcement, but article-body parsing is limited.

cf-kz-bybit-001.legalEntityComparison: All sources concern Bybit Kazakhstan / Bybit Limited local Kazakhstan service context.

kazakhstanCitizens.finding: Strongest confirmed user segment. Current public sources support local registration, KYC and fiat access for Kazakhstan-based users, and an official local campaign page explicitly limits one current promotion to citizens of the Republic of Kazakhstan.

kazakhstanResidents.finding: Public local materials still support a Kazakhstan-residence model: the registration guide still says only KZ residents can register, the individual-KYC guide requires proof of residency within Kazakhstan, and fiat services are limited to Kazakhstan-verified users.

foreignNationalsResidentInKazakhstan.finding: Material support exists but is not fully harmonized. The 2026-01-26 official announcement says Bybit Kazakhstan now accepts users with non-Kazakhstan citizenship for registration and KYC. KYC materials accept foreign-country passport/ID documents, and business-KYC materials also accept residence permits/driving licenses. However, the older resident-only registration guide was not updated, and the individual-KYC FAQ still contains citizenship-based territorial wording.

nonResidents.finding: Not confirmed for the local Bybit Kazakhstan stack. Current public local materials continue to point to Kazakhstan residence and Kazakhstan verification for local registration/KYC/fiat workflows.

acceptedIdentityDocuments.finding: For individual KYC, current official content accepts passport and national ID issued by the user's country of origin, and rejects domestic passports and visas. For business-KYC-associated identity checks, passport, identity card, residence permit and driving license are accepted if issued by the person's country of origin.

proofOfAddressAndIINRequirements.finding: Proof of address generally must be dated within three months. The individual-KYC guide currently says only proof of residency within Kazakhstan is accepted. IIN/BIN matching is explicitly required on local fiat bank-transfer deposit and withdrawal flows. Current public sources do not prove that an IIN is universally required merely to open an account; they prove it for fiat/bank-transfer rails and related verification fields.

cf-kz-bybit-001.unresolvedEvidence: Updated Bybit Kazakhstan registration/KYC article or platform terms expressly defining whether eligibility depends on Kazakhstan residence, Kazakhstan citizenship, or specific Kazakhstan-issued identity/address elements.

cf-kz-bybit-002.legalEntityComparison: Local pages operate in the Bybit Kazakhstan/Bybit Limited/AIFC context; global payment-support pages are broader Bybit.com operational pages and do not always specify local-entity routing.
